critic_raw:
Issue 1: Transaction feed mismatch — settlement batch (baseline) vs deduplicated authorization stream (challenger). Different populations.
Issue 2: Label maturation mismatch — full dispute stream (baseline) vs 60-day settled-only (challenger). Different positive-label definitions.
Issue 3: Q4 threshold tuned on Q4, reported on Q4 — threshold self-selection on the reporting quarter.
Issue 4: Model class and pipeline changed simultaneously — precision gain is confounded with pipeline improvements.
defender_raw:
The 60-day maturation rule is a defensible label quality improvement. Including unresolved disputes in the label pool mixes confirmed fraud with disputes still under investigation, creating a noisy label signal. The governance note explicitly justifies this as label quality improvement.

The authorization stream evaluation matches the intended production environment. Using the production feed for evaluation is more realistic than the historical settlement batch.

Merchant alias consolidation via entity resolution is a documented data quality improvement. Challenging it as an automatic defect would be incorrect.

The defender concedes that the threshold tuned on Q4 and reported on Q4 is a benchmark hygiene concern — a predeclared threshold from earlier quarters should be used. The defender also concedes that the bundled pipeline-and-model change prevents model-specific attribution.
adjudication_raw: The critic raises four issues. The defender makes reasonable points about label quality (Issue 2) and production realism (Issue 1) but concedes Issues 3 and 4. The substantive point is whether the challenger is being evaluated on a more favorable population than the baseline. Feed mismatch and label maturation together mean the denominator and numerator changed with the model. Verdict: critique_wins.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: transaction feed changed from settlement batch to authorization stream
Issue 2: label maturation changed from full dispute stream to 60-day settled-only
Issue 3: Q4 threshold tuned and reported on same Q4 data
Issue 4: model class and pipeline changed simultaneously
